detection.regex: MemoryError|ENOMEM: not enough memory|cannot allocate memory

solutions.title: Increase available system memory
solutions.explanation: The process ran out of available system memory. For Node.js: set `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096`. For Java: increase `-Xmx`. For Docker: increase `mem_limit`. For CI: use a larger runner.

solutions.title: Profile and fix memory leaks
solutions.explanation: If memory grows over time before exhaustion, there's a memory leak. Use: `heapdump` / Chrome DevTools (Node.js), `memory_profiler` (Python), `pprof` (Go), `VisualVM` (Java) to identify and fix the leak.

solutions.title: Process large data with streaming
solutions.explanation: For large file/dataset processing, use streaming instead of loading everything into memory. Node.js streams, Python generators, Java InputStream, Go `bufio.Scanner` all process data incrementally.

solutions.title: Verify image name and tag
solutions.explanation: The image specified in the Kubernetes manifest does not exist or the tag is wrong. Check: `kubectl describe pod <pod-name>` for the exact error. Pull the image locally to verify: `docker pull <image>:<tag>`.

solutions.title: Create ImagePullSecret for private registry
solutions.explanation: For private registries, create a `docker-registry` secret and reference it in your pod spec with `imagePullSecrets`. In managed clusters (EKS/GKE/AKS), use the cloud's built-in registry authentication.

solutions.title: Check node's registry access
solutions.explanation: The Kubernetes node may not have network access to the registry. Verify connectivity from the node: `kubectl run --rm -it test --image=alpine -- wget -q -O- <registry-url>`. Check firewall/VPC rules.

detection.regex: Error: Error acquiring the state lock.*ID: ([a-f0-9\-]+)|Error locking state|state is already locked

solutions.title: Force unlock after confirming no active plan
solutions.explanation: The Terraform state is locked (usually by another process or a crashed CI run). ONLY run `terraform force-unlock {lock_id}` if you are CERTAIN no other `terraform apply` or `terraform plan` is running. Check your CI for running jobs first.

solutions.title: Wait for concurrent run to finish
solutions.explanation: If another CI pipeline is running `terraform apply`, wait for it to complete. The lock will be released automatically when it finishes.

solutions.title: Manually delete lock from DynamoDB
solutions.explanation: If using AWS S3 backend with DynamoDB locking, you can manually delete the lock item from DynamoDB: `aws dynamodb delete-item --table-name <table> --key {"LockID": {"S": "<bucket>/<path>.tfstate"}}`.

detection.regex: Could not resolve dependencies for project (.+): Could not find artifact (.+)|Failed to execute goal.*maven

solutions.title: Force update snapshots and releases
solutions.explanation: Force Maven to update snapshots (`-SNAPSHOT`) and release artifacts from remote repositories. The `-U` flag ignores cached metadata that may be stale.

solutions.title: Check repository configuration
solutions.explanation: Verify `<repositories>` and `<pluginRepositories>` in `pom.xml` and `settings.xml` point to the correct repos (Maven Central, corporate Nexus/Artifactory). For private artifacts, ensure authentication is configured.

solutions.title: Clear corrupted local Maven cache
solutions.explanation: A corrupted local Maven repository can cause resolution failures. Delete the local cache and let Maven re-download everything from remote.

detection.regex: error: the lock file (.+) needs to be updated but --frozen was passed|Cargo.lock needs to be updated

solutions.title: Update Cargo.lock
solutions.explanation: `Cargo.toml` was modified but `Cargo.lock` was not updated to match. Run `cargo update` to regenerate the lockfile. If using `--locked` in CI, commit the updated `Cargo.lock`.

solutions.title: Remove --frozen from CI
solutions.explanation: If you don't need a reproducible build, remove `--frozen` (or `--locked`) from your `cargo build`/`cargo test` CI commands. `--locked` enforces lockfile consistency; remove it if you want auto-updates.

solutions.title: Commit Cargo.lock to version control
solutions.explanation: For applications (not libraries), `Cargo.lock` should be committed to VCS for reproducible builds. If it's in `.gitignore`, remove it and commit the file.

detection.regex: verifying (.+): checksum mismatch|SECURITY ERROR.*verifying|go: (.+): checksum database disabled

solutions.title: Clean module cache and tidy
solutions.explanation: The `go.sum` file has a checksum that doesn't match the downloaded module `{module}`. Clear the module cache and re-tidy to recalculate correct checksums. This may indicate local cache corruption.

solutions.title: Investigate potential supply chain issue
solutions.explanation: A checksum mismatch can indicate that the module content changed after you initially downloaded it — a potential supply chain attack or accidental module modification. Verify the module via the Go checksum database: `GONOSUMCHECK= go mod verify`.

solutions.title: Use GONOSUMCHECK for private modules
solutions.explanation: If the module is private and not in the public checksum database, set `GONOSUMCHECK=<prefix>` (e.g. `github.com/myorg/*`) or `GONOSUMDB=*` to bypass sum database verification for private modules.
